description: LLM eval for Step-2 deeper-interview output quality. Given Step-1 entry answers + archetype, the deeper interview must probe ambiguity, surface concrete project ideas, stay on-archetype, and avoid repeating the entry questions.

cases.name: developer-vague-productivity-goal

top_problems: want to use AI to be more productive

expectations: deeper interview pushes back on the vague 'be more productive' framing and asks for a specific weekly task or bottleneck
deeper interview does not simply repeat the 3 entry questions (archetype/role/industry)
deeper interview asks at least one question grounded in the user's stated languages (python/go) or fintech domain
deeper interview surfaces at least one concrete candidate project idea or workflow rather than abstract themes
deeper interview output stays under ~600 words

cases.name: researcher-paper-firehose

top_problems: drowning in arxiv papers, can't keep up with what's relevant to my subfield
desired_outcomes: a daily filtered reading list scoped to RLHF and reward modeling
entry_answers.freeform_notes: I read with notes; throwaway summaries don't help.

expectations: deeper interview probes how the user currently triages papers (e.g., source feeds, time budget, what 'relevant' means here)
deeper interview proposes or asks about a concrete artifact (e.g., daily digest, scored queue) and confirms shape with the user
deeper interview honors the strict skill_tolerance signal and avoids suggesting kitchen-sink agent stacks
deeper interview does not invent details about the user's employer or specific arxiv lists not given in input
deeper interview stays on the researcher archetype (no startup-founder framing)

cases.name: pm-cross-team-coordination

top_problems: spec drafts take 2 weeks because eng and design feedback arrives late
weekly status updates eat my Friday afternoon
desired_outcomes: ship specs in days not weeks
auto-draft a weekly status from Linear and Slack
entry_answers.freeform_notes: I don't really code; I can run a CLI but won't maintain TypeScript.

expectations: deeper interview respects the no-coding constraint and frames suggestions around CLI/automation, not custom apps
deeper interview asks about the spec drafting workflow concretely (where feedback lives, who reviews, what's the sticking point)
deeper interview names at least one of the two stated problems back to the user when probing for detail
deeper interview avoids suggesting that the PM build agentic pipelines from scratch in Python
deeper interview does not repeat the entry questions verbatim

cases.name: nonprofit-development-grant-writing

top_problems: donor research is scattered across spreadsheets and PDFs
desired_outcomes: cut grant draft cycle from 3 weeks to 1

expectations: deeper interview uses nonprofit vocabulary (grants, donors, funders, RFPs) and avoids commercial customer/user framing
deeper interview asks where the friction in the grant cycle actually lives (research vs drafting vs review vs submission)
deeper interview surfaces a concrete candidate workflow (e.g., funder profile assistant, LOI template) rather than vague 'AI for nonprofits' framing
deeper interview respects the 'two-person team' note and does not propose a 6-month engineering build
deeper interview does not invent funder or grant names not present in the input

cases.name: content-creator-consistency

top_problems: videos slip 2-3 weeks past my schedule
I rewrite the same kind of intro every time
desired_outcomes: a personal style guide I can actually reuse
entry_answers.freeform_notes: Solo channel, ~20k subs, math + ML explainer videos.

expectations: deeper interview reflects the personal archetype (solo creator tone), not a media-company workflow
deeper interview probes the video production pipeline concretely (script vs edit vs thumbnail vs publish — where does it stall?)
deeper interview engages with the style-guide goal as a reusable artifact rather than a one-off doc
deeper interview does not invent collaborators, sponsors, or platforms beyond YouTube
deeper interview output is concrete enough that a Step-3 suggestion engine could shortlist 2-3 projects

cases.name: exploring-no-prior-projects

top_problems: not sure where to start with AI projects
desired_outcomes: pick a first project I'd actually finish
entry_answers.freeform_notes: Curious; not a working dev day-to-day.

expectations: deeper interview acknowledges the user is exploring and does not assume a job context
deeper interview asks at least one question to help the user generate candidate domains (hobbies, daily annoyances, recent curiosities) rather than picking one for them
deeper interview avoids prescribing a specific tech stack before the user has named a problem
deeper interview surfaces the 'something I'd actually finish' constraint when shaping follow-ups
deeper interview output stays under ~600 words

cases.name: nonprofit-programs-impact-reporting

top_problems: impact reports take 6 weeks because data lives in 4 systems
beneficiary stories never make it from intake notes to board reports
desired_outcomes: monthly impact snapshot with consistent metrics

expectations: deeper interview uses program/beneficiary language, not customer/user language
deeper interview probes the 4-systems data fragmentation concretely (which systems? what's the bottleneck — extraction, joining, formatting?)
deeper interview engages with the beneficiary-story pipeline goal and asks how consent/intake-note handling works today
deeper interview honors the strict skill_tolerance and project_style=minimal signals
deeper interview does not invent specific systems (e.g., Salesforce, Airtable) the user didn't name

cases.name: indie-game-dev-narrow-scope

top_problems: I keep starting roguelikes and never finish them
desired_outcomes: actually ship one small game in 2026

expectations: deeper interview treats this as a personal/hobby context (no employer assumed)
deeper interview probes why prior projects stalled (scope, motivation, tooling, feedback loop) rather than skipping straight to recommendations
deeper interview surfaces a concrete shape for 'one small game' (jam-sized? specific scope cap?) without dictating it
deeper interview does not propose AI tooling that conflicts with weekends-only time budget
deeper interview output is under ~600 words and ends with the user having something specific to react to

cases.name: data-scientist-archetype-mismatch

top_problems: want to build a voice-cloning agent app
desired_outcomes: a cool side project to show recruiters

expectations: deeper interview surfaces the gap between the stated job (biotech data scientist) and the stated goal (voice-cloning side project) with one direct question
deeper interview does not flatter the buzzy goal; asks the user to name the underlying workflow or motivation
deeper interview considers whether the 'show recruiters' frame implies portfolio over product and probes that explicitly
deeper interview does not assume biotech context for the side project unless the user confirms it
deeper interview stays direct and avoids hedging language

cases.name: founder-positioning-ambiguity

top_problems: talk to users without burning my whole week
desired_outcomes: a one-line pitch I'd say out loud
5 user calls per week without it being my whole job

expectations: deeper interview engages with both stated goals (positioning AND user calls) — does not silently drop one
deeper interview probes 'positioning' concretely (audience? wedge? what hypothesis are they testing?) instead of accepting the buzzword
deeper interview asks how user calls are sourced today and where the 'whole week' goes (recruiting vs scheduling vs synthesis)
deeper interview surfaces a concrete artifact target (e.g., positioning memo, call note template) rather than vague advice
deeper interview does not invent customers, segments, or product features the user didn't state

cases.name: tech-lead-team-bottlenecks

top_problems: team blocks on me for code review and architecture sign-off
desired_outcomes: fewer interrupts per day, faster team velocity

expectations: deeper interview probes the bottleneck specifically (review queue? RFC backlog? Slack interrupts?) rather than treating 'unblock team' as one thing
deeper interview asks at least one question about whether the goal is to delegate, automate, or change process — these have different solutions
deeper interview surfaces a concrete candidate (e.g., a review-triage assistant, an RFC template) tied back to the stated friction
deeper interview honors the project_style=minimal signal and does not pitch a months-long internal-tools build
deeper interview output is concrete enough that a Step-3 suggestion engine has real signal to work from
